item.title: Sneak peek: Searching for Maya Millete
item.description: CASE UPDATE: A young mother disappears. Investigators say her husband contacted spellcasters to put a hex on his wife so she wouldn’t leave him. "48 Hours" contributor Jonathan Vigliotti reports Saturday, July 18 at 10/9c on CBS and Paramount+.

item.title: U.S. announces new strikes on Iran as war intensifies
item.description: The U.S. announces new strikes on Iran just hours after a fourth consecutive nighttime barrage, as the war ramps back up over control of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Key moments as Todd Blanche testifies before Senate panel for Attorney General confirmation
item.description: Lawmakers pressed Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche on a slew of issues during the first part of his confirmation hearing to lead the Justice Department. CBS News' Caitlin Huey-Burns breaks down the key moments, which included questions about the Epstein files and the proposed "anti-weaponization" fund.

item.title: Heinrich presses Trump nominee Jay Clayton on the release of personal information of Epstein victims
item.description: New Mexico Democratic Sen. Martin Heinrich questioned President Trump's nominee to be director of national intelligence, Jay Clayton, during a Senate panel confirmation hearing about the release of Jeffrey Epstein victims' personal information when he worked as U.S. Attorney for the Southern District of New York.

item.title: Jay Clayton scolded during hearing for lack of preparation on Gabbard role in Georgia election probe
item.description: Georgia Democratic Sen. Jon Ossoff questioned President Trump's nominee for director of national intelligence, Jay Clayton, about who he believed won the 2020 presidential election. Ossoff also brought up Tulsi Gabbard's highly scrutinized presence at an FBI raid on an elections office in Fulton County, Georgia. Also, Democratic Sen. Mark Warner of Virginia, the vice chairman of the Senate Intelligence Committee, scolded Clayton for not being prepared to answer questions about Gabbard.

item.title: California man charged in missing wife's murder left a review for spellcaster
item.description: Investigators say her Larry Millete contacted spellcasters to try to put a hex on his wife Maya so she wouldn't leave him.

item.title: Jay Clayton pressed on subpoenas for journalists in apparent national security probe
item.description: Jay Clayton, President Trump's pick to be director of national intelligence, faced questions about subpoenas issued to journalists regarding an apparent national security matter. Oregon Democratic Sen. Ron Wyden pressed Clayton for more details on the process before the subpoenas were sent.

item.title: NYPD officer says Brooklyn Bridge rescue happened on 3rd day with elite unit
item.description: Cristian Yepes was on his first week with the NYPD's elite Emergency Service Unit when he helped rescue a woman on the Brooklyn Bridge.

item.title: Son of couple jailed in Iran "desperately worried" as stepdad's sentence extended
item.description: A man whose mother and stepfather are imprisoned in Iran tells CBS News "the hardest part is the uncertainty."

item.title: Sen. Whitehouse clashes with Todd Blanche over his relationship with Trump: "It takes 2 to collude"
item.description: Rhode Island Democratic Sen. Sheldon Whitehouse slammed Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche during his confirmation hearing over his relationship with President Trump, accusing the pair of colluding.

item.title: Watch: Todd Blanche refuses to commit to meeting with Epstein survivors, says DOJ staff will
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche is facing a panel of senators for his confirmation process to lead the Justice Department permanently. Blanche refused to commit to meeting with Epstein survivors after Sen. Dick Durbin of Illinois, the top Democrat on the Judiciary Committee, pressed him on the Epstein investigation and the department's role in transparency regarding records.

item.title: Trump's intelligence head pick Jay Clayton gives opening statement at Senate committee hearing
item.description: Trump's pick for director of national intelligence, Jay Clayton, addressed the Senate Intelligence Committee before his confirmation hearing on Tuesday.

item.title: Here's what the Common Cents Act means for businesses, consumers
item.description: New law aims to address how businesses and consumers should transact amid the phase-out of the penny.

item.title: Cyclospora outbreak leaves consumers guessing which foods are safe
item.description: Officials are still searching for the source of the outbreak, prompting consumers to seek advice on social media about which foods to avoid.

item.title: Wildfires expose millions in Midwest, Northeast to dangerous smoke
item.description: Heavy smoke from several large wildfires blazing in Canada and Minnesota is expected to engulf large swaths of the Midwest and Northeast U.S. this week.

item.title: Watch: Lawmaker presses Blanche on his role in alleged Epstein files mishandlings
item.description: Iowa Republican Sen. Chuck Grassley, who chairs the Judiciary Committee, pressed Todd Blanche, the acting attorney general, on the apparent mishandling of the Epstein files during the release process that he led while at the Department of Justice during President Trump's second term in the White House.

item.title: Blanche reiterates "anti-weaponization" fund is "dead" at confirmation hearing
item.description: "It is a moot issue, meaning there is no weaponization fund. The weaponization fund is dead," Blanche told lawmakers on the Senate Judiciary Committee.

item.title: Clayton faces questions about election integrity, vows to enhance trust in DNI
item.description: Clayton, currently the U.S. attorney for the Southern District of New York, is expected to succeed Bill Pulte, Trump's controversial acting director of national intelligence.

item.title: Todd Blanche's opening statement at confirmation hearing touts Trump administration successes
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche delivered his opening statement at the Senate Judiciary Committee hearing to vet him to permanently fill the role in the Trump administration.

item.title: How ICE policy changes could impact the 2026 midterm elections
item.description: A man was fatally shot by an Immigration and Customs Enforcement agent in Biddeford, Maine, during a traffic stop near his home, prompting the agency to make certain policy changes. CBS News' Fin Gomez has more.

item.title: More details emerge on D.C.-area ground stop that left thousands of travelers stranded
item.description: A Tuesday ground stop in the Washington, D.C., area over security concerns linked to Iraqi Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi's visit left tens of thousands of travelers stranded. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi has more.

item.title: Will daylight saving time become permanent after House passes bill?
item.description: Lawmakers are one step closer to making daylight saving time permanent​ after the House passed a bill with overwhelming support.

item.title: Officer who rescued woman on Brooklyn Bridge was in first week on elite unit
item.description: Officer Cristian Yepes helped to save a woman's life on the Brooklyn Bridge last week. It was only his third day on the job with the NYPD Emergency Service Unit. Anna Schecter reports.

item.title: England-Argentina World Cup semifinal reignites painful historic rivalry
item.description: The World Cup semifinal between England and Argentina is a grudge match that will reopen decades-old wounds for both countries.

item.title: Flights grounded at D.C.-area airport due to security concerns for Iraqi leader
item.description: The Iraqi leader was in Washington to meet with President Trump.

item.title: Cop who rescued woman in crisis on Brooklyn Bridge was on his 3rd day with unit
item.description: Warning: sensitive content. Footage released Tuesday shows police officers successfully rescuing a woman who was in crisis on the Brooklyn Bridge. Officer Cristian Yepes, who helped talk the woman off the bridge in what was just his third day with his unit, spoke with CBS News' Anna Schecter about the operation.

item.title: Where's Maya Millete? Family, friends continue search for missing California mom
item.description: A California mother disappears without a trace – did her husband try to have a hex put on her so she wouldn't leave him?

item.title: AI companies are creating "generative ghosts" of deceased loved ones
item.description: Startups are using emails, photos and voice recordings to create AI simulations that family and friends can interact with after a loved one's death.

item.title: Chef Greg Baxtrom serves delicious Midwestern meals to New Yorkers
item.description: Chef Greg Baxtrom is bringing approachable Heartland flavors to New York City with 5 Acres, a Midwestern restaurant in the iconic Rockefeller Center. Baxtrom talks about creativity with vegetables and shares his struggle with sobriety.

item.title: Timeline: The disappearance of Maya Millete
item.description: Maya Millete, 39, went missing in January 2021. What was happening between her and her husband before she disappeared and what does spellcasting have to do with it?
